Combined Review: Broken - Keep getting signed out. Camera sessions “ending” constantly.

Decent, please add Apple Watch support - Positives: aesthetically the interface is nice. Like the layout and graphics. Cons: Clunky, UI glitches, cameras sometimes glitch out, can’t make payments to my day care in the app and have to routed to the website. It’s a, better than nothing, situation. Please add Apple Watch support for notifications and improve the UI fluidity and speed of navigation through the app. It would be beneficial if payments were within app.
Keeps logging me out - Tried changing passwords, deleting, reinstalling, customer support, nothing works!!! Was at another center that used tadpoles for 6yrs, not once did it fail.

We need a SproutAbout web application. - Just as the title says. Not everyone has access to their phones during the work day so being able to keep up with my kids while at my work computer is greatly needed. Considering this app replaced Brightwheel for most child care providers, a web application for SproutAbout should have been a top priority. I hope you all have one in the works.
Delayed - I’ve never been able to open this app without it taking 15 minutes to get to the home page. Worst child care app out there! Avoid if possible!

Punishing Teachers for Bad App - I continue to be disappointed in this app, but this latest “feature” really takes the cake.     Rather than fixing any of the plethora of issues we continue to highlight in reviews and surveys, LCG is now trying to pin the blame on teachers with its weekly rating of each classroom. The issue is not the teachers, and it never has been. The issue is that you created a poorly functioning, non-intuitive app, and rather than accept responsibility or heaven forbid you fix something without making other things worse, you’re trying to blame someone else. The teachers are fantastic. LCG leadership in charge of this app is apparently the problem here.

Works perfectly for me - If you were to go off the “star rating” of this app you’d think it was a train wreck. But for me, it has always worked great.    I can see videos, pictures, and updates of my kid consistently. I can easily message staff and can see they are receiving and answering messages.    Lots of the complaints might be from people trying to use certain unstable features of the app. Receiving updates, images, videos, messages all work great for me though!
Happy - I’m happy to be able to see my grandson.

Very Unreliable - The app very consistently shows messages as sent that never go through. Upon checking back later it either displays an error message at that point letting you know it never sent or briefly pops up in the chat and disappears as if never sent. This leaves critical communication unsent when you are left to otherwise believe it has been effectively communicated. Also, the video streaming portion is very buggy and has lots of issues switching cameras.

The app - This app was designed to make it easier on the teacher and the parents. It has done everything except make it easier on both ends. The app constantly has glitches, interfering with the ability to properly communicate between teachers and parents.

The worst thing - This app doesn’t work well. It keeps freezing, some days it doesn’t even allow me to see that my kid is in. I hear the teachers talk about how it lags in the class. My students teacher doesn’t post as much and she says she can’t because of the timing. With each update it gets worse. Get rid of it! And start again!!! If I could, I would give 0 stars.
Has some bugs - Overall, the app works well enough for me. But I can’t seem to get the “Bulletin” notifications to go away once I’ve read them, which happened after two updates ago.

Needs a UX Review - It would be worthwhile for the company that created the app to invest in contracting with UX and customer loyalty consultants for future version updates. The app is clunky and frequently glitches. Scrolling through pictures can be difficult as it easily returns you to the main page. The presentation of meal, etc. updates is too big and prominent. The info is not personalized to my child as I do not have the option of having the app refer to my child in gender specific pronouns. The screen to talk to the teacher should have an option for a simple message vs comments for drop off and pick up. I have experienced issues with my messages not being received by the teachers, so now I call the school instead of using the app. It seems as if the production of the app and the go live was rushed. The app has potential. Brightwheel has improvement opportunities, but it is currently a much better solution.
App never works - This app always freezes, never works right and now I can’t even get into it.

Please Update - I’m both a teacher and a parent through LCG.   As a parent, i love the app and it’s features.     As a teacher, this app is horrible. It would make it far more easier if teachers were able to have some control over which children are shown in “my classroom”. I’m unsure of other centers, but ours has a bunch of children who can be in that room grouped together. Whenever we have children checked in it doesn’t show them all grouped together, rather spread throughout the list that doesn’t seem as though it has a method of sorting. As a brand new teacher it made it EXTREMELY confusing trying to figure out which children were actually assigned to be in that room on a day to day basis.   Please add the option to edit photos that are taken within the app itself, like if we need to fix the rotation, that is a big one for myself personally.   Please add the option to have an announcement, instead of just Activity/Sleep/ Lesson Plan/Food. If the announcement is added, if possible to make the notification appear differently than the updates that pop up for the child.   I have had a few parents say something about the comments they have left on activity updates, can we as teachers have the ability to reply back to the comments, as well as receive notifications of interactions with the updates.

Not good - My children’s center took away a great working app and from what I understand, replaced it with their own that they created. And it’s terrible. It’s glitchy. It doesn’t send me updates no matter if I have notifications on, reboot, sign in/out etc. the pickup/drop off note section isn’t easy to use and the messages delete after 24 hours. It will randomly kick me out and make me sign back in. Not impressed.

Horrible App - I wanted to update this review to say that the bulletin scrolling issue has been fixed. However, we still have massive issues with notifications. Badges specifically do not work. I will say banner notifications work about 70% of the time, but still… And the school frequently has to alert us that the app is down or not working properly. We were definitely spoiled with Brightwheel and being able to tailor notifications. I know it was easier for staff to take photos too.     The idea of this app is great and, when it works, it’s wonderful. But I stress *when* it works. Since we’ve had the app, there’s been glitch after glitch after glitch. And one glitch - where the bulletins freeze as you attempt to scroll and it takes you five minutes to read a message that should have taken less than one - that glitch has never been addressed. Yes, we’ve contacted the email address about it to no avail. I’ve uninstalled, reinstalled, logged out, back in, yada yada, as has my husband and two other families (both using two phones each) and we all *still* get frozen on bulletins. To the point that I’ve asked the Director if she can just email. Well, she also has to text because - surprise - notifications don’t always work. Bring back Brightwheel please! Please, please, please! We have it for years and never had issues like this. If they could get a competent team to update the app and make it functional, it’d be awesome and I truly thought it was great at first. Not anymore. Now it’s a headache. Please don’t reply telling me to contact support - SIX OF US HAVE and you’ve been unable to fix it.
Broken!! - The App isn’t working. Whatever your update did it broke the app. Fix it!! As this is how the school communicates with us.

Very glitchy and long load times - Not sure why our daycare pays for this app. It’s often glitchy, video and photos have issues when trying to tap to view, and lately I can’t even get the update feed to load. Daycares need to pick a different app developer because this one isn’t worth it.    Edit - welp turns out this app is owned Learning Care Grouo which is owned by a private equity firm who also owns the daycare we send our kid to. Probably means the daycares are captive clients (ie probably forced to use this app). Also means this app will always be terrible because if the daycares are forced to use it because they are both owned by the same parent company there probably won’t be competition from an outside app company…. Thanks for making a terrible app in the name of profit

Glitchy and slow - I feel like I have issues every day with this app. Currently, I can’t even get it to open and load updates. To get the video to stream I usually have to close and reopen the app all together. Hoping newer versions with updates come soon

Inconsistent - App works inconsistently. Sometimes it works with all functions, others it opens but never loads. Sometimes the camera streaming functionality freezes and you have to refresh to re-load it. Messages from parents to teachers don’t always go through. It’s a very clunky app that needs work. It’s frustrating to not be able to follow my children throughout their day or know when my infant has had bottles/naps. Would rather use another app service.

Garbage - This app is garbage. It takes forever to load and often will never load. The teachers have a hard time updating it. The video always freezes. I wish my daycare would switch back to Brightwheel because this app is not usable most days.

Please fix this app - Often, I’ll try to open the app when I receive a notification and the app simply will not load or open. This is unacceptable when this app is the main way our childcare provider communicates with us.

Awful - We use this app as our daughter is in daycare and we like to watch how she’s doing and get updates. However, we can never do that as the app is ALWAYS freezing and we cannot see our daughter. It’s constantly refreshing with that spinning circle and signs us out and/or doesn’t show us anything. This is ridiculous and needs to be fixed asap.
Freezes A LOT!! - After you log in the app freezes for a long time and there isn’t a way to unfreeze it. I tried restarting, uninstalling and reinstalling. Please try and fix it

It’s ok…but needs much work - It’s a decent app…when it works properly. The video section is unbelievably glitchy. Whenever I try to use it I get a note that there are unlimited 30 minute viewing windows which is fine but as soon as you try to view it freezes then you have to fuss with the app to get it to display the message to allow you to view again. This seems to happen lately whenever I try to view video which seems odd. Assuming this is a new addition because that didn’t previously happen. It’s absolutely annoying so I hope this is fixed sooner rather than later. The app also doesn’t always send messages so I echo the sentiment of others on this. I’ve logged in and out and even restarted my phone to see if it resolves the issue but it doesn’t so the issue definitely seems to be with the app itself. Also it occasionally will say there are no active children enrolled on the account but then the app will miraculously show them after a bit more fussing with the app. Not sure what is going on behind the scenes with the app but hopefully the bugs are worked out soon.
Slow - The app is extremely slow and everything takes a long time to load. Switching between messages to updates and video takes a long time and hasn’t improved during the year plus I’ve used it.

Constantly ends session - I really dislike this app. It CONSTANTLY ends my viewing sessions. Sometimes only minutes after logging in to check on my child. I’ll watch for a few minutes and then I have to sign back in to keep watching. Seriously annoying.

Horrendous load times - This app consistently hangs and doesn't load pages. When I tap a notification that my child is tagged in, 9/10 times it does not find the entry. It also fails to load my account a good amount of the time. Image gallery viewing is a simple concept that you don't even think about in most apps, but this one makes a bad experience out of it. Decent concepts for an app but horrible execution.
Latest update - The last update has caused so many lags that I’ve never experienced before. The home page doesn’t load and the video icon doesn’t even load half the time.
JUNK!!!!! - Bring Brightwheel back!!!!! This app is JUNKKKK!!!!!!!! IT NEVER WORKS!!!!!
Won’t even open - I don’t know what’s going on with this app, but it won’t even open for me anymore

Slow, no good - This app is so slow! Most of the time I am having to close the app and reopen just to get it to load. It then still takes forever to load. I want brightwheel back! You can only see the classrooms with this app so when my son goes to the soft play room I can’t have access to the cameras in that room. The cameras frequently just stop as well. I would give this app 0 stars if it was possible.

A slow, buggy mess. - Please hire a competent senior software engineer or two to come in and fix your app. There is no fathomable reason the home page should load so slowly (or often not at all), and the messaging system, one of the most important aspects of an app like this, is still entirely unreliable.

Same complaints as everyone else - App barely loads anymore. Takes at least 30 seconds from the splash screen to boot up. My messages don’t send to the teachers. Cannot switch to another camera once in full screen video because the screen gets stuck. Video constantly freezes and I can’t close out of full screen video to another tab at all. Have to close the app and reopen every time.   Seriously, this app barely functions. Must be a lot cheaper for providers than the alternatives because I can’t otherwise imagine why they’d use it.

App rarely works - If I could give this zero stars I would. The app often does not load. I’ve checked for updates and I have the last version. I’ve deleted and redownloaded and still have an app that fails to load.

Just “okay” - This app needs some improvements.  1. The videos lag all the time  2. It continually states “no children” when mine are at daycare. This could be the app side or the daycares side. I know they have glitches on their side with checking children in on the app a lot.  3. Lag in posts. I scroll back through and see things posted days prior that we’re not posted on that day.  4. Overview is great when it works, I wish you could see a weekly overview and that not reset at midnight every night.
Slow - This App is super slow, opens half the time and other half just sits at the initial screen when you open the App. Don't mind the layout and navigation, but it's just painfully slow.
Slow - I find opening and closing the app is very slow and for things to load can take forever
Too many freezes - This is irritating. I had to delete the app and reinstall it several times. And I still can’t go pass the into/logo. Like why???!!

Glitches - This app is terrible. The idea is great but it constantly glitches and won’t load.  I saw I got a notification that I had a message from the teachers but I cannot get message to load.   I keeps saying my daughter is no longer assigned to the class (she is).  This app is constantly glitching like this.   By this point you would think they could have this up and running better than it is.

Useless app & site - Watch Me Grow and Brightwheel were great whether stateside or from overseas to watch my kiddo. Sprout About is useless. Doesn’t load, picture is small and when you try to enlarge it only enlarges one side of picture. Video is basically non existent.   La Petit - bring back the live feed camera in clad rooms and we’ll all be happy.

I'm a teacher who has to use this app - This newest Update is not great at all. my messages to my parents are not always sent, and that is so frustrating. Only about a 3rd of my parents are getting my weekly information board.   It's slow and logs me out at random. Clocking children in and out is a pain because it lags.   Sending daily photos and updates takes me over an hour because of lagging.   I'm hoping, the next update is an improvement.

Glitchy - I can’t get bulletins to load at all. And messages only loads half the time. It won’t sign me out so I just keep deleting the app and reloading and hoping it’ll work this time but it doesn’t. I miss brightwheel.
Consistently has issues loading or populating - The app is constantly crashing, not reliable whatsoever.

Garbage App - This app is terrible and I hate that I am forced to use it to o keep up to speed with what’s going on with my kiddo. I think id be better off just calling the daycare.  The camera feature is buggy and often won’t close unless I force quit the app.  The app often takes more than a minute to open or switch tabs.  Tabs frequently open with no associated information unless I go to a different tab and then back (see previous point).  I don’t get notifications when my SO posts messages, only when employees respond to her.  Similarly, school employees aren’t notified when other employees respond.  Messaging is so segmented that employees organized in different groups (admin, classroom1, classroom2) can’t see each other’s messages.    In short, it’s slow, buggy, and poorly thought out.  If your daycare uses this app, I recommend finding a new daycare.
Terrible - Doesn’t work, unstable, full of bugs.

No staff message indicators and other shortcomings - There are no sticky alerts for staff messages. If you miss the push notification, you might miss important messages like, “your child needs more wipes” or “they’re sick” unless you check constantly yourself. This is either a bug or bad user interface design (I have every notification enabled). Messages are grouped by date, all of which are labeled “today”. You have to look at the individual timestamps. Staff cannot see messages once they are logged out. This is a step down from other apps like brightwheel. 2023/01/19 - timeout when loading on their latest version. Right now having the app is as good as not having it. < 1 star if I could.     1/19/2023 - “a timeout error has occurred. Please try again”. I’d really rather not…I’d rather be able to just use the app the first time… great update!    1/30/2023 -ongoing app slowness and timeouts. Latest version.
App won’t open - Frozen on the splash screen since the latest update
New Update - Downloaded the new update for this app, now it will not open. I think there’s something wrong with this new update
Can never see photos - I hate this app! I can always see that the teachers have posted photos, but can never view them. Doesn’t matter if I’m on Wi-Fi or not
Problems - Having issues with adding people to the Care Circle other than that, works great.
Doesn’t meet my needs - I miss brightwheel.
Missing functionality - I don’t get notifications when my child is checked in and out. The time out is too fast on the camera.   When you make the picture full screen you can’t zoom in

It’s Ok - Needs improvements - I am a software tester in my career. This app has gotten to the point of being functional but needs a few improvements to make it as good as competitors.     1) The app is slow. Needs some additional allocation of resources to run faster and likely some code cleanup for various calls that take too long.   2) Saving photos and videos takes too many clicks.   3) directly messaging staff doesn’t exist in a clear format on this application. Having an inbox to message the school to tell them something that they can just reply back to cuts down on calls and let’s them reply when they have the time. You have a drop off and pick up location for this which is fine but needs stronger alerting on their end so they always see it. And allow comments on pictures and such. But strictly a messaging inbox to communicate would be huge.   4) Access on a computer would be nice.     Many of the other bugs people mention I haven’t experienced for a couple of months now so I think in that respect things are improving. Just threw out the main areas for improvement instead of small nit picking ones.

Cannot scroll back in time for photos/videos - I attempted to scroll back to September to view photos/videos, however after going back one month the feed just turns white. We no longer have access to see memories of our kids growing up and reflect on how they have progressed.     With Brightwheel we were able to filter by dates. Additionally, we were able to view these on a computer web browser which made saving/downloading much easier.

Photo and video saving features need improvement - Please allow filtering videos and pictures by date. Also enable auto download for them. App crashes when I want to review older pictures. Sometimes saving video requires multiple clicks.

Just ok - Video freezes frequently. Also, if you play a video that was posted on the main feed and then continue to scroll along the feed, the video will continue to play, sometimes playing 2 audios overlapping each other. Difficult to scroll the posted photos quickly; opens another window which then you have to close out. Inconsistently receive messages (my husband will receive but I won’t) and we’re not always sure the teachers receive our messages. I do like the summary feature at the top and how I can see each of my children.
Camera issues - Issues with cameras. Usually after an update. Currently haven’t been able to view for days.

Seems like a beta release - The app is slow, glitchy, and often times not responsive. It takes the app a while to load photos, refresh the feed, and update messages. I get logged out frequently for no reason at all. The slowness makes the app mostly unusable. The app is so slow and glitchy it feels like it’s in a beta test mode. If it is indeed in beta testing we should all get paid as being testers/ devs.

Comparing to Watch Me Grow - My issue with with both of these sites is their problem with viewing on iPad. It’s meant to be used only on the phone… also Watch Me Grow used 2 cameras whereas Sprout About only accesses one.   Sprout About does give way more info and photos

Getting better by the Week - A solid App, great to have everything in one place. Don’t get discouraged by the low reviews from when it was first deployed. Lots of functionality improved and bugs worked out by now.

Live video access restricted - Please give the school leadership the ability to reinstate live video access. I mistakenly took a single screen shot and my video access was removed. I am being told by our schools director that she is unable to reinstate and there is no options for appeal. She has asked that I get my video reinstated but does not know the path to take to request this. I understand the app says no screenshots when you first log into the app, but I have written a statement that it was a mistake and included the screenshot. Nothing was done with malicious intent. But there is no way to request an appeal. Who can I communicate with to request reinstatement?

Great app! - Our daycare started using this app recently and the user interface is pretty great! But more than that, the company is very open and responsive to feedback. I shared some ideas that were quickly implemented, and they regularly reach out to their community for feedback. It’s been a great experience so far!

Not bad - I don’t know Brightwheel as context but so far I only had one issue logging in and the developer responded within a day and that was resolved. The rest of the app provides what I need and I haven’t had any other issues. I have confidence in the developer as they do respond to the reviews, so that is a positive sign. I have noticed that I can access the app through a VPN. This makes me a little uncomfortable as I feel this should not happen for security reasons.
Enough with the social agenda - My daughter is a girl and she ate her meal not their meal. This is not the place to be push your social agenda. These are little kids we are talking about.
Peace of Mind - I love the ability to see my baby at any time of my day. Love the communication features with the staff, and makes notifications immediate!

Love this app - Despite previous reviews, I am able to communicate with my daughter’s center with a response in a timely manner. I can check in on her in her classroom and I get pictures and lesson plans throughout the day! I love it. Sounds like some of the issues are with communication from the centers themselves and user related.

OK app. - I love the idea of seeing my little one in daycare. The live feed gives me a sense of comfort…when it works. I usually have the app open all day to watch the live feed here and there and every single time it either freezes or times out or the app closes completely. I wish the live feedback was more reliable and less laggy.

Getting Better - Although I somewhat agree with the “Disaster” Review, I do have to say that it is getting better and the staff are getting better at responding. The one thing that I do hope is that it is Apple Watch compatible so I can look at the updates from my watch while I’m working.

Unreliable at best - This app is ridiculous. I want to go back to brightwheel! I almost never get updates. Can’t open it half the time. The only feature I like about it, when it works, is that I can view livestream directly from the app instead of having to open watch me grow

Very buggy when scrolling through photos - This app is OK. It has a lot of great functionalities, unfortunately the app itself is extremely buggy. Whenever I try to scroll through photos, it’s like the app doesn’t register what I’m trying to do. It is very lagging and freezes all the time. It does weird things constantly. They need to hire a better developer to fix all of these ongoing problems.

Your newest update kicked me out - Your newest update completely broke the app. It forced signed me out and will NOT let me back in even though I verified the login info with my kid’s school and with tech support to make sure it was correct. I sign in with my correct email and password, and enter the information, only to get a “data entry does not match our records” message every time. Im still waiting to hear from tech support about the issue because no one knows why this is happening. I also even tried uninstalling and reinstalling with no luck. Your app stinks:(
A lot of unhappy Karens - Never had any issues with the app. I get daily updates and photos and I’m able to access the cameras. It’s been easy to use and quite functional.

Update Causes Streaming Crash - The recent update causes an app crash when streaming on my iPad (the camera “loads” for about 10-15 seconds before crashing). Streaming on my phone is not an issue, but the iPad streaming worked fine until the most recent update.    Please review and fix. Thanks.

App functions extremely poorly. - Quite simply, this app is horrible. Everything loads slowly if it loads at all. Constantly have to close and reload the app to make anything appear on the app. Often when you tap on a notification from the app it comes up to a blank SproutAbout screen. Almost everyday I have to to re-check mark the notifications I would like to receive because they constantly get uncheck marked on their own. I don’t get notifications at all about check ins or check outs. I honestly can’t say anything positive about this app sadly. I miss the brightwheel app, it was stable and reliable. SproutAbout has been nothing but a headache for everyone who uses it. This app either needs to be scrapped completely, or redone from the ground up. This is alarmingly bad for an app that daycare centers rely on for communication back between their staff and parents. I’m not one to write reviews let alone really negative ones, but this needed to be said.     An update to my review 10-25-2022: The app is improving but one of my biggest concerns still remains, we need to be receiving notifications about check ins and check outs from the app. Yes, we can go into the app and look manually to see when they’ve been checked in and out but, I would like to see real time notifications when they get checked in and out like we did with Brightwheel.
Message Badges - We need badges for unread messages. Communication is impossible.

Worst preschool app - Compared to brightwheel this app is terrible. The response is super late. We get updates on any photos and activities like the next day! Or we would constantly have to refresh the app to get any updates/ notification of our child activity. I have no idea why this preschool select this app and would need to prob bring it up to the schools director to seek another application. It’s frustrating using this app.

Livestream has said disabled for weeks- still not resolved - So the school leadership has reached out to SproutAbout about my profile saying disabled she was informed 48 hours this was false, then I speak with 3 different people asking me the same basic questions and saying I will get a call back. Never do just someone knew asking me if my app is up to date and still having issues I say yes it’s up to date and yes it’s still shoveling disabled. They informed the daycare that it was resolved which it has not been… and they informed her they have a very small staff… I actually liked the app we used within the daycare prior I never had any issues. Brightwheel was amazing to use as well as WatchMeGrow. But for some reason SproutAbout is an issue for not only us parents but for the staff as well.
Livestream needs improvement - The livestream is always down.

Not functional for communication - Its been months, close to a year, since our daycare started using the app and communication between classrooms/leadership and parents is STILL not functional. The updates referenced aren’t actually working (or haven’t solved the actual problems). Its incredibly that such critical communication is useless and the speed at which tech support is working to resolve is snails. I would prefer that our daycare stop wasting money on useless technology and actually communicate.

Love SproutAbout! - This app is great! I’m a huge fan of having the interactions with my child’s teachers and having our live-streaming camera reviews all in one. Also love that I can communicate directly with my child’s teacher and school leadership separately. I’m a big fan!

Echoing the other review - Omg please give us brightwheel back. It had its issues but omg SproutAbout is so bad! It regularly logs me out and then tells me the info my school gave me doesn’t match even though it does. I have to call and put in tickets almost every month just to wait weeks for a reply back from someone who doesn’t know or care, just for them to tell me it has to be “escalated.” How many times does it need to be escalated before someone does something? We are on 6, does it need to be ten? Cameras do not flip over to the correct rooms, pictures disappear, security issues are all over including sending push notifications to my phone when I’m logged out and can’t get back in! This means that a abusers who were on your kids profile then were removed can still be getting notifications about where your kid is in the facility and what they are doing! When they aren’t even in the app! What is the logging in even for? This is a huge security violation especially for those with estranged spouses or domestic abusers. On top of that, today a month’s worth’s of messages just poof disappears! I hate this app. It’s the worst.
Horrible app - This app is the worst no wonder the low rating. Camera freezes constantly. Updates don’t load anymore.

Still not working - I’ve been trying to get my login issues fixed through customer service for close to three weeks now. I haven’t been able to login in at all through the app this entire time. Incredibly frustrating experience and no one has any answers.

Excellent App, Highly Recommended - Easy to use and well-organized app layout. Have used this app at two different daycares and the only issue user-based, regarding whether the center staff is updating it regularly. Incredibly easy to learn, and great way to communicate with my child’s care providers throughout the day. 10/10

Slow Tech Support - For years we used watch me grow and brightwheel at childtime. Since switching there has been nothing but glitches in the app. The worst was when we switch to another school, Everbrook that uses sprout about. It took a week, with multiple phone calls to tech support, plus calling my old school to obtain my old guardian id (which isn’t used to log in to the app). So for a week I couldn’t see my children’s at their new school. Then when they added my children I still can’t communicate with the teachers, despite the ticket being “elevated”. While I get the notifications for messages I can’t message the teachers or answer their messages. So I’ve made another phone call and another ticket and it still isn’t resolved.
Doesn’t work - Terrible app. Terrible customer service.

Terrible, inept tech support - My kids were enrolled in a daycare that used SproutAbout in Washington state. We then moved to Pennsylvania, to another daycare that also uses the app. When we tried to login via our family code at the new daycare, the app did not work. We called tech-support and they opened a ticket for us and told us that they would be in contact with us when the issue was fixed. It’s fine - Our daycare used a different app prior to Sprout About which I preferred for user ability and intuitive functioning. I am a fan of icons (ex: landing page is Kids Names), then within that icon in the form of easily clickable categories: nap, food, absent, late drop off, early pick up, messages, notes, etc (versus a drop down menu), then once clicked we see the specifics. I HATE scrolling pages and having to piece together their days from the top of the page down. So jumbled. Basically I only use the app if the daycare tells me there is something on there (notifications don’t even work), but not really for information sharing or checking on my kids’ days like it’s intended.

Major issue with messaging/communication!! - Moving from Brightwheel to SproutAbout has been a difficult adjustment. The home page is visually pleasing with color-coded, big boxes for activities (blue for food, purple for potty, etc). It’s nice that live streaming is incorporated into the app. That’s the extent of the positive feedback I have.     Where the app fails SPECTACULARLY is communication between parents and the teachers. One would think that the note icon that’s second from the left at the bottom of the homepage would be where you can message the teacher/staff, but that’s actually where you write drop off notes. It would make much more sense for that to be where you can communicate with the school. Instead, you have to click the icon at the far right (that I think is supposed to be a text bubble, but it’s hard to decipher and should be changed). When you click the unidentifiable icon, you’re taken to a list of communications from the school. NOWHERE on that page is a button to press to start a conversation. After much frustration, I realized that you need to click the name of their classroom and that’s how you can write a message to the teacher. Before I figured that out, we clicked the message thread from School Leadership since they were the only ones we’d received messages from initially, and when you send School Leadership a message, they don’t actually get it.     From what the staff at our school is saying, the app is clunky and not user-friendly from their end just like it is on our end too. Please fix the messaging setup! Keep the paper and pencil icon on the bottom and allow us to click that, choose who we want to message from a drop-down box, then have the text box underneath that. That eliminates the unidentifiable icon on the far right and it eliminates the need for separate drop-off notes because you’d just type in what you want the teachers to know just like you would a normal message (which is all drop-off notes are).     If you can’t or won’t fix this issue, then do like other apps do and have a tutorial that pops up when you first get the app and anytime you open it until you select to opt out of the tutorial. Have arrows that highlight where to click to find certain information so you don’t throw parents and staff into a deep pool and expect them to figure out these aspects of the app — aspects that don’t make sense on a practical level.     I think it has potential to be a good app, but only if feedback is heard and implemented because if not, we’re all going to be left desperately missing Brightwheel and you’ll be left with a low rating and frustrated parents.

You weren’t ready to launch this app, and the fact that you did anyway and are now impacting all parents across your 1,000+ says a lot. FIX IT.    Concerns:    > Push notifications - there is a toggle in settings to select whether to receive them or not. There was also a request from iphone asking if I would like to enable push notifications from SproutAbout - both were marked ‘on’, however I get ZERO notifications. If my child’s teacher has something important to communicate, I have no idea unless I’m going in every 30-60 minutes to check, which is an unreasonable ask.    > Videos - half the time do not provide the audio (there is always audio being recorded, the app just isn’t providing it).    > daily activity updates - I don’t need graphic art that takes up a full phone screen for each diaper, nap, or food notification. I want to be able to see the notifications summarized. Please reference brightwheel. I shouldn’t have to scroll down 7-10 phone lengths to view the notifications, and then have to try to figure out if I scrolled too far and ended up in the previous day or not.    > How many messaging sections do you think we need?? - In settings, there should be an option to combine all messages into one view instead of having to select from several to see all messages. No idea how this app got launched in this state, but now I know why all the other feedback for this app is so negative.

Not a Fan - My child’s childcare facility switched from Brightwheel to SproutAbout a couple weeks ago and I have not had the best experience with this app. I have only been able to successfully use it like 3 times. I will get notifications from the daycare and when I try to open the app it just loads/cycles forever and never opens. I have uninstalled and reinstalled the app and still have the same issue.

Looks nice but not efficient - Our daycare switched to the app a few weeks ago from Brightwheel and immediately had complications. There are still a lot of technical glitches but beyond that, the functionality is lacking when it comes to communication and visibility. Because of the issues with the app, we’ve been instructed to use three different platforms at once to interact with the school, and that just feels chaotic and not sustainable.   While the UX looks nice, it’s not a reliable solution for the daycare staff to communicate with parents or each other and that is a non-starter for me as a parent.

Learning Care Group should be embarrassed with themselves - From the moment I opened the app it’s been nothing but problems. I am mind-blown over how poorly developed this app is and how shoddily it was rolled out. There were issues right from the get-go with logging in. The interface is deplorable - not efficient at all or easy to use. The cameras? What cameras? They don’t work. Really and truly, Learning Care Group should be ASHAMED of themselves for rolling out such a disaster. The reason I chose my daycare facility was largely due in part to the app-access it offered - and I feel blind-sided that they would do such a bait-and-switch. They took a perfectly functioning system (Brightwheel) and offered some half-baked, poor excuse for an app in its place. It’s truly despicable - this app deserves a solid 1 star. I wish I could give it a 0.

Glitchy and loss of functionality - The daycare staff reports that it’s super glitchy and they don’t get messages. I can’t seem to find a way to send a general message that isn’t drop-off/pick-up which is one of the features I used most for my daughter who has special needs. The camera feed is hit or miss. I also can’t seem to subscribe to notifications for more than a day so I have to go in each day and click the boxes saying I want all notifications. It makes no sense that the app wouldn’t just save my preferences without clearing them each day. I like the idea of the app and that it combines brightwheel and “watch me grow” but the execution is flawed right now. I feel like the previous apps worked much better. I’m hoping the developers can fix these issues soon.

Not too happy - I wanted to love this app seeing how I’d be able to go to one centralized place for updates and live videos. However, most of the time the cameras aren’t working. WatchMeGrow didn’t give me this many issues.

Garbage - Our daycare just switched from Brightwheel and I hope they go back. We can’t get one parent into the app and it’s been weeks. The one we can get into the app is at best a 1 star. Hope they switch back. Get it together.

Development Sorely Needed - This is definitely not what I expected with the new role out. Brightwheel was a lot more functional. But currently I have two children enrolled into Children Courtyard but I only have access to one. I haven't/can't receive notifications on behalf of my first born and it am not aware of any communications until I pick him up. I've submitted a ticket and worked with my facility manager and there hasn't been any progress.

Nonfunctional - Doesn’t save any changes, doesn’t get rid of keypad so you can select the button at the bottom, have to keep force quitting to get it to work. Have had it for an hour and already wishing we could go back to Brightwheel. It is awful.
Horrible! Worst app ever - Nothing works on this app

Horrible - Ever since our daycare switched to this app it has been a nightmare. Can’t see the camera most of the time, delayed updates from caretakers, and overall not reliable. Please fix this app ASAP.

If I could give 0 stars… - For those of you saying that you’re having trouble receiving notifications or accessing the camera, I am jealous. I can’t even get the SproutAbout app to open on my phone for the last 2 weeks. Any time I click the app icon, the SproutAbout splash screen shows up for a brief second and then I get the spinning loading wheel for an eternity. I have tried closing the app and restarting it with no luck. I have tried restarting my iPhone in order to connect and that will work maybe 50% of the time. I’ve reported this to our school, but it sounds like from the recent app reviews, a fix is unlikely to be seen any time soon.

No message notifications is a huge problem - I am not getting notifications when I get messages from teachers. It’s clear teachers are not getting notified of my messages. My ability to communicate with staff has worsened with the rollout of this app (and I’ve always had great interactions with staff via brightwheel - it’s not the staff). I have missed health-related messages and teachers have missed allergy-related questions and requests. It’s completely unacceptable.
Notifications - I don’t get any notifications when things are posted. I’ve missed important communications.

:( - This app does not make it easier for teachers. Classes are fast paced and this app does not allow you to operate in a fast pace classroom. I understand what they were trying to do. However, it’s not working.

Looks nice, functions terribly - Our daycare just switched to this app and so far, not so good. While the interface looks much nicer, the app is very confusing and also buggy. Push notifications are hit or miss (which is problematic when this means you may not see important messages about your child), the messaging section is very confusion (multiple tabs, different threads that often have the same or similar messages) and the live camera integration is horrible. The cameras often do not show and when they do, they glitch out often. This is a major step backwards for us and I hope the app is either quickly updated pr our daycare shifts back to Brightwheel and Watch Me Grow.

The worst application and deserve zero star - My child’s daycare switched to this application few weeks ago (not sure the reason they did that). This is a terrible application regardless the customer service is pretty good. Every week, users have been experiencing dysfunctional livestream. My husband’s and my account have been swapped many times. This is a relatively new application but poorly developed and below acceptance scores to release (not even close to the sale point to daycare centers)

Needs improvement - Loved the idea of having video feed for my toddlers daycare center room. However, this almost never loads. Just all around very buggy and disappointing.

Go back to WatchMeGrow - This app is not working half of the time. The video didn’t work for days, it took forever to load, and if the video is on the screen blacks out after every 15 seconds. This app just defeats the purpose of having access to watch my child at the daycare.

I hate this app… - My center switched over to SproutAbout a few weeks ago from brightwheel, I thought it looked pretty cool… we had training on this app so I figured I’d have no issues with it. WRONG - although I’ve figured my way around it, it doesn’t compare to the ease of brightwheel, not to mention I can no longer message parents out side of work, so if I’m on my lunch break I can’t do anything because I don’t carry around my work iPad, with brightwheel I was able to do everything, any where, any time THAT is my biggest complaint… aside from it checking me out of classes just because I sat the iPad down and left it for a period of time (because as most people know we can’t just sit on our iPads all day) I don’t care for change however I can adapt, this is not something I want to adapt too unless they make some changes. Hopefully these reviews are read and taken into consideration on ways to improve the app.

Awful - Daycare switch to this from Brightwheel and so far it is a massive downgrade. You have to search and look around for any update. From what I’ve noticed so far is the search filters reset anytime you close the app so you have to go in and manually select your children every time of you want to see any updates. Not impressed and pretty disappointed.

Rolled this out before they should have… - Our daycare switched from Brightwheel recently, no doubt to save some money I’m sure, but we’ve had nothing but problems since that happened.     This app has not worked correctly since the beginning- the message feature tells me there’s no child attached to my user account even though I can see the updates for her throughout the day. As a result I can’t message the center throughout the day with any questions of feedback the way I could with Brightwheel. We don’t receive the bulletins from the school. The cameras frequently don’t work on the app, and our child’s teachers don’t seem able to see our comments on pictures or updates. This app has severely diminished my visibility into my child’s day and it’s aggravating.     We’ve relayed these experiences to our daycare and they advised tickets have been submitted but it’s been WEEKS and nothing has improved. It’s not fair that the daycare staff have to use this glitchy app and field parents’ frustrations instead of focusing on their jobs. It’s absurd how much money we spend on daycare and they can’t even ensure the app they’re building works. 0/10 would not recommend.

Horrible. Try finding a school that doesn’t use this app - The whole point of choosing a school with live stream cameras is to watch your kid. SproutAbout makes that impossible. Bright wheel and watch grow worked 10x better.     Have fun logging in and out, and deleting and reinstalling all day just to watch your kid for 5 minutes before it shuts down… real fun.

Live streaming doesn’t work for days - Pros: NOTHING that I can think of!    Cons: Not user friendly. Can message at multiple places. Confusing. I don't see the purpose of drop off and pick up notes. What a crock of crap - Nothing but false hope. Cameras never work on app and the daycare blames the app that it’s not a problem on their end. iPad compatible - Could you please make this application IPad compatible. This application is only iPhone compatible.

There’s a reason why companies pay for Brightwheel. - Sure it’s a Beta launch. Having your own proprietary technology may be beneficial to a large corporation like LCG. That being said, the amount of utter frustration that has been voiced from every end of the spectrum in possible users of this product shows, without a doubt, that LCG should cut their losses & go back to what worked. In layman’s terms; “if it ain’t broke, don’t fix it”.     No matter what fixes they put into this, their Beta roll out has tainted this product beyond repair. The things that need to be fixed should’ve never been an issue in the first place. Don’t be cheap. Pay the subscription to Brightwheel. Parents hate it. Teachers DEFINITELY hate it. Management hates it. The upper echelons of LCG have no issue with it im sure, which are the same people who designed such a terrible interface in the first place.

Terrible - The apps we had before this was so much better. Way too many bugs. It logs me out at least 3 times a day. I try to watch my baby on the camera and it won’t work. So ever time it logs me off i have to look up her IDs on my phone to login and it’s SUPPPER slow.

Very slow and buggy - The app is very slow, I have to restart it many time when it freezes. Notifications comes randomly with a big delay or sometimes never show. The messaging system is also very slow, and sometimes I need several attempts for my messages to get delivered. Video is super slow to load and sometimes freezes as well.

No sound notifications - App works fine but it doesn’t make sense that there are no “sounds” when notifications are sent through the app. I would think this is a pretty important feature. Please fix!!
Useless - My daycare just switched to this app and though I like 1 app instead of 2, it rarely loads and then times out. It needs to be reworked.

Bad Operation and useless Customer care - Even with correct guardian and family Id, I cannot login. I am told by customer care that they just launched 100 schools and they can’t resolve my issue immediately. If you can’t handle urgent request what is the use of a call center. There is no supervisor to talk. This is important for me since it’s my kids first day and we need to see how is he doing. Very irresponsible management

Needs Better User Experience - Our daycare center just switched to this app about 3 weeks ago from Brightwheel, and so far I do not think it has as great of a user experience. A few frustrating experiences I’ve noticed so far… The app lacks notification functionality for notes and the manager thread. As a parent, I want to be notified of all things posted in the app, and not have to go searching for what’s new. Also, the camera functionality doesn’t always load, I have to log out and log back in, there should be a refresh option within that section of the app. I also think the bulletin should have functionality for parents to react to a bulletin. I understand why the development team chose not to allow message functionality on bulletins (I don’t love it), but we should be able to react or show engagement. Lastly i would be interested to know if the drop off and pick up notes are commonly used by users, we haven’t used it at all and only seems relevant for infant classrooms so i don’t think it should be in the main navigation panel at the bottom… it could be a sub page in messages. All in all — I hope our center goes back to BRIGHTWHEEL

Not User Friendly - I am a teacher who uses this app. I was very excited for its arrival. Disappointed now it’s arrived. Developers I hope you see this feedback and change some of the problems with it. I really wanted to like this app. It’s glitchy and often times won’t post or load. The set up and functions of the app are complicated. There are too many options and it makes it difficult to find the simple options we need. The classroom is a fast-paced environment. It makes me not want to use the app because all of the additional steps I have to take. For example, I always have to select a time rather than it applying automatically. I also have to select diaper/toilet rather than it applying automatically and changing based on need. I can write “loose bm” in the notes. I do not need an extra box for that. When entering food it doesn’t provide you with a section to add menu items. Sure, we can write it in the notes but it is frustrating when before I could just add it and search it later. The app looks crowded and busy. There is a lot of words on one page at any given time. I recommend a bigger focus on the children's picture and more visually diverse symbols. It is also difficult to tell what children are checked into my room at any given time. I find hard to check an individual child’s profile to see if they were changed or fed because the emphasis is on words not symbols. The symbols brightwheel uses makes it quick and effective. The same can’t be said about SA. My last complaint is communication via the app. There are simply too many places for parents to send messages and it is hard to catch them all. Did they send an actual message? Was it a drop-off note? Maybe a comment on a picture? Children change rooms multiple times and it is frustrating that all staff isn’t receiving all messages from all parents all of the time. Communication was much more effective when everyone was able to read messages. Floaters, for example, teachers who change rooms multiple times a day, I think will struggle the most from this. One more additional thing, drives me nuts that the screen doesn’t rotate vertically. I can’t enter in info with one hand because it only displays horizontally. The classrooms are fast-paced environments and sometimes I don’t have access to both hands. My job is to supervise, love and take care of children. Using SA I have spent more time than usual staring at an iPad trying to get/enter basic info about my kids. It also makes it complicated to take pictures of the kids because horizontal is a wider shot than vertical. We have to capture pics of individual children without revealing the identity of their classmates. Kids typically play really close to each other so a wider shot is not ideal. I hope these are issues you can fix in later versions of the app. I hope you receive enough teacher feedback and these things change. I wanted to like this app. I am 26 and I feel so bad for older teachers who aren’t tech savvy. If I am experiencing these difficulties and feel dread everytime I have to log something, I can only imagine how they feel.
